opportunities.title: Awesome Foundation Micro-Grant
opportunities.blurb: Local volunteer chapters worldwide give out $1,000, no-strings grants to any project they find worth funding. Apply anytime — no deadline, no niche restriction.

opportunities.title: Pollock-Krasner Foundation Grant
opportunities.blurb: Unrestricted funding for professional, actively-exhibiting painters, sculptors, and artists working on paper — materials, studio rent, or living expenses. Rolling applications open worldwide; review takes 9–12 months.

opportunities.title: PhotoVogue Global Open Call 2026: Brave New Visions
opportunities.blurb: Free global photo and video open call from Condé Nast's PhotoVogue, judged on original visual storytelling. Three cash grants from $2,000–$6,000, plus festival presentation and a shot at Vogue publication.

opportunities.title: FCA Emergency Grants
opportunities.blurb: Rolling grants for US-based experimental visual artists, poets, and performers with a confirmed but unfunded upcoming show. Apply 6–10 weeks before your presentation date.

opportunities.title: Illuminations Grant for Black Trans Women Visual Artists
opportunities.blurb: A $10,000 award, plus four $1,250 finalist prizes, for self-identified Black trans women and trans femmes working in visual art in the US. Application fee is waived.

opportunities.title: Monthly Micro-Grants for Podcasters
opportunities.blurb: Monthly grants up to £1,000 for individual podcasters worldwide, with priority for disabled, LGBTQIA+, and racially minoritized creators. The current window opens July 17 and closes July 31, 2026.

opportunities.title: Esports World Cup Creator Program
opportunities.blurb: Co-stream Esports World Cup and Esports Nations Cup 2026 matches to earn from a $2,000,000 pool of rewards, with top performers winning a trip worth up to $50,000. Reward eligibility starts around 50 average concurrent viewers.

opportunities.title: Razer Creator Program
opportunities.blurb: Razer's always-open creator program: affiliate commission up to 5%, product discounts, an exclusive Discord, and quarterly gear giveaways, with tiers starting below 5,000 followers. No cash sponsorship is guaranteed at entry tiers; 18+.

opportunities.title: AXS Film Fund 2026 Open Call
opportunities.blurb: Grants of up to $10,000 for documentary filmmakers and nonfiction new-media creators living with a disability, at any stage of production. The 2026 open call closes July 31; no letter of inquiry required.

opportunities.title: Artist Project Grant (Summer 2026)
opportunities.blurb: Project grants of up to $15,000 for emerging artists making melody- and lyric-driven music — alt-folk, americana, bluegrass, indie rock, R&B, soul. Applicants must be eligible to work in the US with no more than three full-length albums released.

opportunities.title: Gottlieb Emergency Grant
opportunities.blurb: One-time emergency assistance for painters, printmakers, and sculptors hit by a catastrophic event like fire, flood, or a medical emergency. Requires at least ten years working in a mature phase of your art; a $5,000 award is typical.

opportunities.title: Craft Emergency Relief Fund
opportunities.blurb: Rolling emergency relief grants of $3,000 for craft artists who make original three-dimensional work by hand — pottery, glass, textiles, jewelry, wood, metal. Requires a US-based, public-facing practice for at least the last two years.

opportunities.title: Investigative Reporting Grants
opportunities.blurb: Grants of up to $10,000 for freelance journalists and outlets pursuing investigative stories, plus $1,000–$2,500 seed grants for early-stage reporting. Full proposals need a letter of commitment from a news outlet; seed proposals don't.

opportunities.title: Visual Arts Grant (Summer 2026)
opportunities.blurb: Project grants of up to $10,000 for visual artists, including photographers, who have lived in New York City for at least one year. The cycle closes August 3, 2026 or once 40 applications arrive — whichever comes first.

opportunities.title: Open Fund for Individuals
opportunities.blurb: Rolling fund for freelance and self-employed artists, writers, producers, and other creative practitioners living in Scotland. Supports research, development, or delivery of creative projects lasting up to 24 months.

opportunities.title: PPL Momentum Music Fund
opportunities.blurb: Grants for UK-based artists and bands ready to break through to the next career level. Funds recording, touring, and marketing activity; the current round closes August 10.

opportunities.title: Research & Development Award (Individuals)
opportunities.blurb: Research and development awards for artists and creative professionals whose work happens mostly in Wales. Small awards run to £10,000 and large awards to £30,000, covering up to 90% of costs.

opportunities.title: Music Project Award 2027
opportunities.org: The Arts Council / An Chomhairle Ealaíon
opportunities.blurb: Supports specific artistic projects in music for individual artists and organisations in Ireland. Part of the Arts Council's Project Award 2027 round; applications close July 23.

opportunities.title: UK–IRL Culture Connects Grant
opportunities.blurb: Helps emerging artists in the UK and Ireland collaborate in person and develop new work across the two countries. Individual artists apply through the British Council Ireland site.

opportunities.title: FACTOR Live Performance Program
opportunities.blurb: Tour and showcase support for Canadian artists with a FACTOR profile and a recent or upcoming release. Support ranges from CA$7,500 in showcase funding up to CA$37,500 combined for top-rated artists.

opportunities.title: BIPOC Deaf Arts Grant
opportunities.blurb: Five $1,000 grants for US-based BIPOC artists who identify as Deaf, DeafBlind, DeafDisabled, or Hard of Hearing, across literary, media, performing, tactile, and visual work. Applicants must be 18 or older.

opportunities.title: RE:Focus Fund Post-Production Grants
opportunities.blurb: Post-production grants of up to CA$2,000 for shorts and CA$3,000 for features written and directed by women, non-binary, and/or trans creators telling 2SLGBTQ+ stories. Rough-cut stage projects are eligible worldwide.

opportunities.title: Sony World Photography Awards 2027
opportunities.blurb: Free-to-enter global photography awards across Series ($25,000), Single Image ($5,000), Student, and Youth competitions. The 2027 edition is open now; the professional Series competition closes January 12, 2027.

opportunities.title: CEWE Photo Award 2027
opportunities.blurb: Free worldwide photo contest open to everyone from smartphone shooters to professionals — enter up to 100 images across 10 categories. Around 1,000 winners share roughly €250,000 in prizes; entries close May 31, 2027.
